page: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512_vnni and AVX512_bf16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is provisioned with 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and is premium storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and memory performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average range. The PassMark memory score is strong, placing in the top 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth remains average. For language model workloads, the server delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small 135M models, though performance drops to average for medium 7B models and weak for large 70B models.

With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, this server offers a specialized hardware profile designed for memory-intensive applications. The inclusion of a substantial 3780 GB local disk provides high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads that require massive RAM without scaling up vCPU counts. The server is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32 GB per vCPU. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, this x86_64 server includes 3780 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong PassMark memory scores. While multi-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth are average, the server excels at small-model LLM text generation. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by providing massive RAM capacity without requiring excessive vCPU scaling. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, massive memory capacity, and high-capacity local storage for demanding data workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads
